title: Pithivier
subtitle: Round puff pastry pie filled with almond cream
excerpt: Two discs of puff pastry sealed around a rich almond cream, scored in a spiral pattern, and baked until puffed and lacquered with golden glaze. Serve warm. Serves 6–8.
sourceNotes: The pithivier originates in the town of Pithiviers in the Loiret, France, where it is documented from the seventeenth century as a regional speciality. Auguste Escoffier, Le Guide Culinaire (1903), includes it among the standard puff pastry entremets. The form consists of two discs of puff pastry encasing a filling (traditionally almond cream or frangipane), with a crimped edge and a spiral scored on the top. It is closely related to the Galette des Rois baked for Epiphany.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan, adding 10–15 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble and refrigerate for up to 24 hours before baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 210°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: The characteristic curved scoring on a pithivier is made with the tip of a sharp knife, starting at the centre and curving out to the edge in an arc — then repeated at even intervals around the disc to create a spiral fan pattern. Do not cut all the way through the pastry, only score the surface about 2 mm deep. The scoring allows the top layer to expand in the oven without buckling or tearing. Chill the assembled pithivier for 20 minutes before scoring so the pastry is firm enough to hold a clean line.

content.text: The almond cream must be fully chilled before assembling — warm cream will soak through the pastry before it sets in the oven. Seal the edges firmly and chill the assembled disc before the
. The glaze on top caramelises in the oven to give the lacquered finish.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale. Beat in the {{eggs}} (cream amount) one at a time. Fold in the {{almond-flour}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{almond-extract}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Chill for at least 30 minutes until firm.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the {{puff-pastry}} and cut two 22 cm discs. Place one disc on a lined baking tray. Spread the chilled almond cream over the base disc, leaving a 2 cm border. Brush the border with beaten {{eggs}} (wash amount). Place the second disc on top and press the edges firmly to seal. Crimp the edge with a fork or the back of a spoon. Chill for 20 minutes. Brush the top with egg wash. Score the top surface in a spiral fan pattern with a sharp knife, going no deeper than 2 mm. Make a small hole in the centre for steam to escape. Bake for 30–35 minutes until risen, deep golden, and firm. Cool on a rack for at least 15 minutes before serving warm.
